questions.q: What is the primary objective of accounting?
questions.answer: The primary objective of accounting is to provide financial information about a business to its stakeholders.

questions.q: Accounting is a process of collecting, recording and classifying financial data of an enterprise. Which one of the following is not a step in the accounting process?
Distinguish between the terms 'account' and 'record' in the context of accounting. Explain with the help of an example.
questions.answer: An account is a self-balancing summary of all changes in value for a related group of assets, liabilities, and equity of a business. It is a representation of all debits and credits that have been posted in the ledger. For example, a sales account is a record of all sales made by a business. A record, on the other hand, is a document that contains a list of transactions related to a particular account. For example, a sales journal is a record of all sales made by a business.

questions.q: Fill in the blank: Accounting is a language of ______________.
What is the main difference between a nominal account and a personal account?
questions.answer: A nominal account is an account that appears in the trading and profit and loss account of a business, whereas a personal account is an account that is related to a person or a business entity.

questions.q: True or False: Accounting is a mandatory process for every business.
What is the purpose of a ledger in the accounting process?
questions.answer: The purpose of a ledger is to provide a permanent record of all transactions of a business in a systematic and organized manner.

questions.q: What is the difference between a journal and a ledger?
questions.answer: A journal is a book of original entry in which all transactions are recorded in the order in which they occur. It is a temporary account that is used to record all transactions of a business. A ledger, on the other hand, is a permanent record of all transactions of a business. It is a collection of all accounts of a business and is used to provide a permanent record of all transactions.

questions.q: Fill in the blank: The accounting equation is also known as the ______________.
What is the purpose of accounting standards?
questions.answer: The purpose of accounting standards is to provide a framework for accounting practices to ensure consistency and comparability in financial reporting.
